explicit_text: I consider Wilmington, Delaware my home because I lived there from about age 5 to 14. Even though I was born in Philadelphia, I spent my formative years around Wilmington. Later in life, I met my copilot Walter Krohl, who was also from Wilmington, which made our bond stronger. Wilmington was also the city of some of the boys I traveled with to military training.

explicit_text: I remember Pearl Harbor Day very vividly. I was at home in Viola, Delaware, with my brothers when we heard the news around 1:30 or 2:00 in the afternoon. We were shocked and alarmed, listening to the radio all evening, and the general feeling was that we were going to retaliate strongly. I was about 16 or 17 years old at the time.

explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army on February 19, 1943, after initially trying to join the Navy.
